# Phase 10.0.18 Legacy Stewardship Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, deployment authority, autonomous governance, APIs, background jobs, dynastic governance systems, hereditary-authority automation systems, or execution authority.

## Objective

Preserve constitutional legacy, intergenerational stewardship, future-generation responsibility, custodianship survivability, and civilization continuity across centuries after existential safeguard doctrine.

## In-Scope Documents

- Constitutional legacy-preservation doctrine.
- Intergenerational stewardship philosophy.
- Civilization continuity-across-centuries architecture.
- Institutional inheritance survivability standards.
- Constitutional future-generation responsibility doctrine.
- Stewardship continuity-under-time philosophy.
- Civilization guardianship survivability framework.
- Anti-legacy-corruption doctrine.
- Constitutional continuity-beyond-generations architecture.
- Institutional custodianship survivability standards.
- Phase 10.0.18 legacy stewardship summary.

## Validation Checklist

- No runtime activation exists.
- No execution authorization exists.
- No deployment authorization exists.
- No provider onboarding exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No infrastructure activation exists.
- No legacy-preservation layer creates runtime authority.
- No stewardship framework creates dynastic or hereditary authority.
- No custodianship framework enables hidden operational control.
- No future-generation responsibility claim weakens auditability.

## Review Notes

- Stewardship means accountable preservation, not inherited authority.
- Legacy continuity preserves source doctrine, dissent, failure memory, assumptions, and future review.
- Custodianship records must identify scope, limits, uncertainty, and prohibited authority.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, OAuth, APIs, fetch/axios, Prisma/Supabase changes, background jobs, autonomous governance, dynastic governance systems, hereditary-authority automation systems, or execution systems.
